sections.title: Database

Database specifies the database name

Persistence configures database storage
Enabled enables persistence
Size specifies the storage size
StorageClass specifies the storage class (empty for default)
Port specifies the database port
SSLMode specifies the SSL mode for builtin DSN
Username specifies the database username
External configures connection to external database
Database specifies the database name
Host specifies the database host
PasswordSecret references the secret containing the password
Key is the key in the secret
Name is the name of the secret
Port specifies the database port
SSLMode specifies the SSL mode for connection
Username specifies the database username
Type specifies the postgres database type: builtin, or external

sections.title: Sandbox Node Placement
sections.description:
SandboxNodePlacement configures the shared node placement used by
sandbox workloads and node-local sandbox services.

entries.description: SandboxNodePlacement configures the shared node placement used by sandbox workloads and node-local sandbox services.
NodeSelector constrains sandbox workloads and node-local sandbox services onto a specific node set.
Tolerations allow sandbox workloads and node-local sandbox services to run on tainted sandbox nodes.
The pod this Toleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.
Effect indicates the taint effect to match. Empty means match all taint effects. When specified, allowed values are NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule and NoExecute.
Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys. If the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys.

Operator represents a key's relationship to the value. Valid operators are Exists, Equal, Lt, and Gt. Defaults to Equal. Exists is equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a pod can tolerate all taints of a particular category. Lt and Gt perform numeric comparisons (requires feature gate TaintTolerationComparisonOperators).

TolerationSeconds represents the period of time the toleration (which must be of effect NoExecute, otherwise this field is ignored) tolerates the taint. By default, it is not set, which means tolerate the taint forever (do not evict). Zero and negative values will be treated as 0 (evict immediately) by the system.
Value is the taint value the toleration matches to. If the operator is Exists, the value should be empty, otherwise just a regular string.

sections.title: Service: edgeGateway
sections.description: EdgeGateway configures the edge-gateway service (control plane)

entries.description: EdgeGateway configures the edge-gateway service (control plane)
Config contains edge-gateway specific configuration
AuthMode controls how human-facing authentication is handled. Allowed values: "self_hosted", "federated_global".
Base URL for OIDC callbacks
Built-in Authentication
AdminOnly restricts built-in auth to admin accounts only
AllowRegistration allows new users to register
EmailVerificationRequired requires email verification
Enabled enables built-in email/password authentication
Cache configuration
Identity and Teams
Edition: "saas" or "self-hosted"
Server configuration
Internal Authentication
OIDC Providers
OIDCProviderConfig configures an OIDC provider.
AutoProvision automatically creates users on first login
ClientID is the OAuth client ID
ClientSecret is the OAuth client secret
DiscoveryURL is the OIDC discovery URL (.well-known/openid-configuration)
Enabled toggles the provider
ID is the unique identifier for the provider (e.g., "github", "google")
Name is the display name
Scopes are the OAuth scopes to request
TeamMapping configures automatic team assignment
DefaultRole is the role assigned to new users
DefaultTeamID is the team to add users to
Domain filters users by email domain
Timeouts
Rate limiting
RegionID is the canonical region identifier used by tenancy and routing contracts, for example "aws/us-east-1".
Scheduler configuration (optional, for multi-cluster mode)
Enabled enables or disables the service
Ingress configures ingress settings
ClassName specifies the ingress class name
Enabled enables ingress
Host specifies the ingress host
TLSSecret specifies the TLS secret name
Replicas specifies the number of replicas
Resources specifies resource requirements

Claims lists the names of resources, defined in spec.resourceClaims, that are used by this container. This field depends on the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate. This field is immutable. It can only be set for containers.
ResourceClaim references one entry in PodSpec.ResourceClaims.
Name must match the name of one entry in pod.spec.resourceClaims of the Pod where this field is used. It makes that resource available inside a container.
Request is the name chosen for a request in the referenced claim. If empty, everything from the claim is made available, otherwise only the result of this request.
Limits describes the maximum amount of compute resources allowed. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/

Requests describes the minimum amount of compute resources required. If Requests is omitted for a container, it defaults to Limits if that is explicitly specified, otherwise to an implementation-defined value. Requests cannot exceed Limits. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/
Service configures the Kubernetes service
Port specifies the service port
Type specifies the service type

sections.title: Service: manager
sections.description: Manager configures the manager service (data plane)

entries.description: Manager configures the manager service (data plane)
Config contains manager specific configuration
Autoscaler config for pool scaling behavior
MaxScaleStep caps the maximum pods to add in a single scale operation.
MinIdleBuffer is the minimum number of idle pods to maintain above minIdle. When idle count drops to minIdle + MinIdleBuffer, proactive scaling kicks in.
MinScaleInterval is the minimum time between scale operations for a template. This prevents thundering herd when multiple cold claims arrive simultaneously.
NoTrafficScaleDownAfter is the duration without any claims before scaling down. Scale down is still async and happens in the background reconcile loop.
ScaleDownPercent is the percentage to reduce replicas during scale down.
ScaleUpFactor determines how aggressively to scale up. When cold claim occurs, newReplicas = current * ScaleUpFactor.
TargetIdleRatio is the target ratio of idle pods to active pods. Formula: desiredIdle = active * TargetIdleRatio

Enabled enables ingress

NodeSelector constrains netd onto a specific node set. Deprecated: use spec.sandboxNodePlacement.nodeSelector instead. This field remains as a backward-compatible alias when the shared placement is unset.
Replicas specifies the number of replicas
Resources specifies resource requirements

Claims lists the names of resources, defined in spec.resourceClaims, that are used by this container. This field depends on the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate. This field is immutable. It can only be set for containers.
ResourceClaim references one entry in PodSpec.ResourceClaims.
Name must match the name of one entry in pod.spec.resourceClaims of the Pod where this field is used. It makes that resource available inside a container.
Request is the name chosen for a request in the referenced claim. If empty, everything from the claim is made available, otherwise only the result of this request.

RuntimeClassName specifies the Kubernetes runtime class for the netd daemonset. Use a host-compatible runtime such as runc. Do not run netd on gVisor or Kata.
Service configures the Kubernetes service
Port specifies the service port
Type specifies the service type

Tolerations allow netd to run on tainted sandbox nodes. Deprecated: use spec.sandboxNodePlacement.tolerations instead. This field remains as a backward-compatible alias when the shared placement is unset.
The pod this Toleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.
Effect indicates the taint effect to match. Empty means match all taint effects. When specified, allowed values are NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule and NoExecute.
Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys. If the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys.

Operator represents a key's relationship to the value. Valid operators are Exists, Equal, Lt, and Gt. Defaults to Equal. Exists is equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a pod can tolerate all taints of a particular category. Lt and Gt perform numeric comparisons (requires feature gate TaintTolerationComparisonOperators).

TolerationSeconds represents the period of time the toleration (which must be of effect NoExecute, otherwise this field is ignored) tolerates the taint. By default, it is not set, which means tolerate the taint forever (do not evict). Zero and negative values will be treated as 0 (evict immediately) by the system.
Value is the taint value the toleration matches to. If the operator is Exists, the value should be empty, otherwise just a regular string.
